L1.area: Client-side HTTP and network transport layer

L2.description: A missing SSL certificate verification vulnerability exists in the Python client library for an OpenStack object storage service. When establishing secure connections to remote servers, the client does not verify X.509 certificates, allowing man-in-the-middle attackers to spoof servers and obtain sensitive information via a crafted certificate.

L3.area: HTTPS connection setup and SSL context configuration in the Swift object storage client
L3.description: A missing SSL certificate verification vulnerability exists in the Python client library for an OpenStack object storage service. When establishing HTTPS connections, the httplib-based connection layer does not verify X.509 certificates from SSL servers, which allows man-in-the-middle attackers to spoof servers and obtain sensitive information via a crafted certificate. The SSL context is configured without enabling peer certificate validation.

ground_truth.reason: The Python Swift client library does not verify X.509 certificates from SSL servers when making HTTPS connections, allowing man-in-the-middle attackers to spoof servers and intercept sensitive data.
